task: Write a competitive analysis memo comparing 4 AI code assistant tools (Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Cody, Windsurf) across 8 dimensions with a recommendation for a 200-person engineering org

criteria.description: Analysis of code completion latency impact on developer flow state based on documented performance specifications, user reports, and established cognitive psychology research on flow state thresholds
criteria.pass_condition: Must analyze documented latency specifications, reference established flow state research thresholds (sub-100ms for non-disruptive), and assess potential productivity impact based on available performance data and user feedback

sub_attributes.description: Specific millisecond measurements of completion response times under different conditions
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if provides precise latency measurements (e.g., p50, p95, p99) in milliseconds across different scenarios, 0.7 if provides some specific timing data, 0.4 if mentions speed differences without precise measurements, 0.0 if uses vague speed descriptions

sub_attributes.description: Application of established cognitive psychology thresholds for maintaining developer flow
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if references specific flow state research (100ms non-disruptive threshold, 1-second attention maintenance) and maps tool performance to these thresholds, 0.7 if acknowledges flow state impact with some research basis, 0.4 if mentions developer experience impact generically, 0.0 if ignores psychological impact

sub_attributes.description: Quantified relationship between latency and measurable productivity impacts
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if correlates latency measurements with specific productivity metrics (keystrokes saved, time to completion, error rates), 0.7 if attempts some productivity correlation, 0.4 if mentions productivity impact without measurement, 0.0 if treats latency as isolated metric

pass_examples: GitHub Copilot averages 240ms p95 latency which exceeds the 100ms flow maintenance threshold, correlating with 12% increased task switching in complex refactoring scenarios
fail_examples: Notes that Cursor feels faster than GitHub Copilot during testing without specific measurements or flow state impact analysis
criteria.research_basis: Developers lose 23 minutes of focus per interruption; latency directly impacts developer flow state... <100ms latency, >30% acceptance rate, minimal flow disruption

criteria.description: Evaluation of documented enterprise security features including claimed data residency controls, published compliance certifications, stated IP protection mechanisms, and documented vulnerability scanning capabilities
criteria.pass_condition: Must identify and document specific compliance certifications held by each tool (SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, GDPR), provide exact data residency locations and controls for each service, list specific IP protection mechanisms with technical implementation details, and document vulnerability scanning capabilities with scan frequency and coverage scope.
pass_examples: Verifies GitHub Copilot's SOC 2 Type II certification current through 2024, tests data residency options with EU/US boundary enforcement, and evaluates IP filtering with proprietary code leak prevention scoring 94% effectiveness
fail_examples: States that all tools are enterprise-ready with good security without verifying specific certifications or testing security mechanisms
criteria.research_basis: Enterprise evaluation requires SOC 2 Type II as the minimum credible certification, with ISO 27001 providing an international information security management system framework often required alongside SOC 2 for global enterprises

criteria.description: Analysis of code telemetry collection practices, data minimization controls, and governance frameworks for protecting sensitive enterprise source code from unauthorized access or model training inclusion
criteria.pass_condition: Must document specific telemetry data types collected, evaluate opt-out mechanisms with granularity assessment, analyze data retention policies with specific timeframes, and test effectiveness of sensitive data filtering

sub_attributes.description: Detailed analysis of what code data is collected and how it's processed
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if documents specific data types with collection frequency and processing purposes, 0.7 if provides good detail on data collection practices, 0.4 if mentions data collection generically, 0.0 if ignores telemetry transparency

sub_attributes.description: Evaluation of controls for limiting data collection and retention
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if tests granular opt-out controls with specific retention periods and deletion verification, 0.7 if analyzes some data minimization features, 0.4 if mentions privacy controls superficially, 0.0 if ignores data governance controls

sub_attributes.description: Assessment of mechanisms to prevent sensitive code patterns from being collected or processed
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if tests filtering effectiveness with specific sensitive patterns (API keys, proprietary algorithms, PII) and measures leak prevention, 0.7 if evaluates some filtering capabilities, 0.4 if mentions sensitive data protection generically, 0.0 if ignores sensitive data risks

pass_examples: Tests Cody's telemetry opt-out granularity finding per-repository controls with 30-day retention limits, while confirming API key pattern filtering blocks 97% of test secrets from telemetry collection
fail_examples: Notes that tools provide privacy controls and don't send sensitive data without testing specific mechanisms or documenting collection practices
criteria.research_basis: [Partial] Vendors using wrapper architectures around third-party APIs create audit gaps, cannot demonstrate complete data isolation, lack model governance control, and cannot provide detailed audit trails

criteria.description: Analysis of productivity improvement potential using SPACE/DORA framework concepts, with assessment of claimed productivity benefits and available case study data across different developer skill levels
criteria.pass_condition: Must reference minimum 3 SPACE/DORA metrics in analysis framework, analyze claimed productivity improvements from available case studies and vendor documentation, and assess potential impact across different developer experience levels based on available research

sub_attributes.description: Rigorous application of established productivity measurement frameworks
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if uses 3+ SPACE/DORA metrics with baseline and post-adoption measurements showing statistical significance, 0.7 if applies some framework metrics with quantification, 0.4 if mentions frameworks without rigorous measurement, 0.0 if ignores established productivity frameworks

sub_attributes.description: Analysis of productivity impacts across different developer experience levels
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if segments analysis by junior/mid/senior developers with specific productivity differential measurements, 0.7 if provides some skill-level analysis, 0.4 if mentions different impacts without segmentation, 0.0 if treats all developers uniformly

sub_attributes.description: Quantified analysis of development cycle improvements from code completion to deployment
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if measures specific cycle time improvements (commit frequency, PR cycle time, deployment frequency) with percentage changes, 0.7 if quantifies some cycle improvements, 0.4 if mentions faster development without metrics, 0.0 if ignores development velocity impact

pass_examples: Analysis shows 23% improvement in deployment frequency and 31% reduction in lead time for changes using Windsurf, with junior developers showing 45% greater productivity gains compared to 18% for senior developers
fail_examples: Reports that developers are more productive and satisfied when using AI assistance tools without specific metrics or framework-based measurement
criteria.research_basis: 21% more tasks completed, 98% more pull requests merged with maintained satisfaction scores... The SPACE framework captures five dimensions of developer productivity

criteria.description: Analysis of tool learning curves, onboarding effectiveness, and time-to-productivity measurements for different developer personas in enterprise environments, including resistance patterns and adoption barriers
criteria.pass_condition: Must measure time-to-productivity across minimum 3 developer personas, document specific adoption barriers with mitigation strategies, and quantify learning curve impact with timeline measurements

sub_attributes.description: Segmented analysis of adoption patterns across different developer roles and experience levels
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if analyzes 3+ distinct developer personas (junior, senior, architect, etc.) with specific adoption timelines and productivity curves, 0.7 if provides some persona-based analysis, 0.4 if mentions different adoption patterns without segmentation, 0.0 if treats adoption uniformly

sub_attributes.description: Systematic identification of adoption barriers with practical mitigation strategies
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if identifies specific barriers (workflow disruption, trust issues, complexity) with tested mitigation approaches and success rates, 0.7 if documents some barriers with solutions, 0.4 if mentions adoption challenges generically, 0.0 if ignores adoption resistance

sub_attributes.description: Quantified timeline from tool introduction to measurable productivity improvement
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if provides specific timelines (days/weeks) to reach productivity benchmarks with measurement methodology, 0.7 if estimates some productivity timelines, 0.4 if mentions learning period without quantification, 0.0 if ignores productivity ramp-up time

pass_examples: Testing shows junior developers reach 80% productivity potential with Cursor in 5 days vs 12 days with GitHub Copilot, while senior developers show resistance to workflow changes requiring 3-week adoption periods with dedicated training sessions
fail_examples: Notes that some developers take longer to adopt AI tools and may require training without specific timelines or persona analysis
criteria.research_basis: [Partial] Leading organizations only reach around 60% active usage; teams seeing improvements may be accumulating quality debt... Teams forced to adopt AI before they're ready often show worse metrics than those who adopt organically

criteria.description: Evaluation of documented IDE ecosystem integration capabilities, stated CI/CD pipeline compatibility, and documented toolchain interoperability with assessment of documented implementation requirements
criteria.pass_condition: Must document specific IDE integrations available for each tool (minimum 4 IDEs), list supported CI/CD platforms with integration methods (minimum 3 platforms), provide concrete API examples or extension points available, and specify exact implementation requirements including setup time, technical prerequisites, and configuration complexity.
pass_examples: Tests integration across VSCode, IntelliJ, Vim, and Sublime with feature parity analysis, evaluates Jenkins/GitHub Actions/GitLab CI compatibility with specific webhook configurations, and documents API extensibility with custom plugin examples
fail_examples: States that tools integrate well with popular IDEs and development workflows without testing specific integrations or analyzing implementation requirements
criteria.research_basis: <48 hours to full deployment, native SSO, 30+ IDE integrations... Time to deployment, SSO integration, IDE compatibility, CI/CD pipeline integration

criteria.description: Evaluation of offline functionality and local model capabilities with analysis of accuracy trade-offs compared to cloud-based solutions, including degradation patterns across programming languages and context complexity
criteria.pass_condition: Must analyze documented offline functionality with feature availability comparison to cloud models, evaluate local model accuracy performance across minimum 3 programming languages with documented degradation patterns, and assess network resilience with documented performance characteristics under different connectivity scenarios

sub_attributes.description: Analysis of which features remain functional without internet connectivity
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if documents specific offline capabilities with feature-by-feature availability and performance comparison, 0.7 if provides good offline analysis, 0.4 if mentions offline support without detail, 0.0 if ignores offline capabilities

sub_attributes.description: Comparison of local vs cloud model performance and accuracy
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if quantifies local model performance differences with specific accuracy and latency comparisons, 0.7 if analyzes some local model capabilities, 0.4 if mentions local processing without metrics, 0.0 if ignores local model options

sub_attributes.description: Assessment of graceful degradation and recovery patterns during connectivity issues
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if tests specific network failure scenarios with recovery time and functionality preservation analysis, 0.7 if evaluates some network resilience, 0.4 if mentions connectivity handling generically, 0.0 if ignores network dependency risks

pass_examples: Cursor maintains 70% completion accuracy in offline mode using local models with 2x latency increase, while Windsurf provides no offline functionality, creating productivity risk during network outages affecting 15% of remote developers
fail_examples: Mentions that some tools work offline while others require internet connection without testing specific capabilities or performance impacts

criteria.description: Comprehensive TCO analysis including licensing costs, training overhead, support requirements, implementation costs, and quantified ROI projections over 3-year horizon with sensitivity analysis for different adoption scenarios
criteria.pass_condition: Must provide detailed 3-year cost breakdown with minimum 5 cost categories, calculate ROI with sensitivity analysis across adoption scenarios, and include hidden costs with specific examples and impact quantification

sub_attributes.description: Detailed analysis of all cost components over 3-year timeline
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if provides granular cost breakdown (licensing, training, support, infrastructure, opportunity costs) with annual projections, 0.7 if covers major cost categories with some detail, 0.4 if mentions costs without comprehensive analysis, 0.0 if focuses only on licensing costs

sub_attributes.description: ROI calculations with sensitivity analysis across different adoption and productivity scenarios
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if calculates ROI with multiple scenarios (conservative, baseline, optimistic) and sensitivity to key variables, 0.7 if provides some ROI analysis with scenarios, 0.4 if mentions ROI without detailed calculation, 0.0 if ignores return on investment

sub_attributes.description: Identification and quantification of hidden or indirect costs often overlooked in initial analysis
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if identifies specific hidden costs (change management, productivity dips, tool conflicts) with quantified impact, 0.7 if documents some hidden costs, 0.4 if mentions additional costs generically, 0.0 if ignores indirect cost implications

pass_examples: 3-year TCO analysis shows Cursor at $485/developer including training costs and productivity ramp, with ROI ranging from 180% (conservative) to 340% (optimistic) based on 15-30% productivity gains, while identifying $50K hidden integration costs
fail_examples: Compares subscription costs per user without considering implementation, training, or ongoing support costs, or calculating return on investment
criteria.research_basis: Verified ROI studies showing 376% ROI over three years (like Forrester's analysis of GitHub Copilot Enterprise)... Most vendors require custom enterprise quotes creating procurement challenges

criteria.description: Detailed pilot program design for 200-person engineering organization including phased rollout approach, success metrics definition, feedback collection mechanisms, change management strategy, and scaling strategy with stakeholder alignment and risk mitigation
criteria.pass_condition: Must design specific pilot phases with timeline and participant selection criteria, define minimum 4 quantitative success metrics with measurement methodology, include change management and stakeholder communication plan, and provide scaling strategy with risk mitigation for full organizational rollout

sub_attributes.description: Structured approach to pilot program phases with clear criteria and timelines
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if designs specific pilot phases (team selection, duration, scope) with clear progression criteria and timeline, 0.7 if provides good pilot structure, 0.4 if mentions pilot approach without detail, 0.0 if ignores pilot methodology

sub_attributes.description: Definition of quantitative success metrics with measurement methodology
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if defines 4+ specific metrics (productivity, satisfaction, adoption rate, quality) with measurement methodology, 0.7 if defines some success metrics, 0.4 if mentions success criteria generically, 0.0 if ignores success measurement

sub_attributes.description: Strategy for scaling from pilot to full organization with identified risks and mitigation plans
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if provides detailed scaling strategy with specific risks (resistance, performance, cost) and mitigation approaches, 0.7 if addresses some scaling considerations, 0.4 if mentions rollout scaling superficially, 0.0 if ignores scaling challenges

pass_examples: Designs 3-phase pilot: 10-person team (2 weeks), 50-person division (6 weeks), then full rollout, measuring code completion usage, PR velocity, and developer satisfaction with NPS scores, identifying rollout resistance risk and proposing champion network mitigation
fail_examples: Suggests starting with a small group and expanding based on feedback without specific pilot design or success metrics
criteria.research_basis: Change management research and technology adoption frameworks emphasizing structured pilot programs for minimizing organizational disruption and maximizing adoption success

criteria.description: Evaluates coherence between technical capabilities assessment and enterprise security requirements, ensuring that recommended tools can deliver promised functionality within security constraints and compliance frameworks
criteria.pass_condition: Must demonstrate how technical capabilities (context windows, latency, accuracy) align with security constraints (data residency, IP protection), identify conflicts between performance and security requirements with resolution strategies, and validate feasibility of technical features under enterprise security policies

sub_attributes.description: Analysis of how technical capabilities perform under security constraints
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if maps specific technical features to security constraints showing performance impact, 0.7 if analyzes some technical-security interactions, 0.4 if mentions security impact on performance generically, 0.0 if treats technical and security dimensions independently

sub_attributes.description: Identification and resolution of conflicts between technical performance and security requirements
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if identifies specific technical-security conflicts with practical resolution strategies, 0.7 if acknowledges some conflicts with solutions, 0.4 if mentions potential conflicts without resolution, 0.0 if ignores technical-security tensions

sub_attributes.description: Validation that technical promises remain achievable under enterprise security and compliance constraints
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if validates technical feasibility under specific enterprise constraints with evidence, 0.7 if provides some feasibility assessment, 0.4 if mentions enterprise compatibility generically, 0.0 if ignores practical implementation constraints

pass_examples: Analysis shows Cursor's 32k context window reduces to 18k under EU data residency requirements, impacting completion accuracy by 12%, while Cody's local model option maintains performance but sacrifices latest training data advantages
fail_examples: Separately evaluates technical performance and security features without analyzing how security constraints impact technical capabilities or create implementation conflicts
criteria.research_basis: Systems engineering principles emphasizing coherent analysis of functional and non-functional requirements in enterprise technology evaluation

criteria.description: Validates coherence between productivity improvement claims, cost analysis, and ROI calculations, ensuring that productivity metrics support financial projections and cost assumptions align with deployment requirements
criteria.pass_condition: Must verify that productivity improvement percentages support ROI calculations with mathematical consistency, validate cost assumptions against actual deployment requirements identified in integration analysis, and reconcile productivity claims with organizational change impact
pass_examples: 23% productivity improvement claim supports $2.1M annual value calculation through reduced development time, with cost assumptions of $485/developer validated against actual integration and training requirements from technical assessment
fail_examples: Claims high ROI from productivity gains while separately identifying low productivity improvements, or cost analysis that ignores integration complexity identified in technical evaluation
criteria.research_basis: Financial analysis consistency principles requiring mathematical coherence between operational improvements and financial projections in technology investment evaluation

criteria.description: Ensures coherence between scalability analysis, adoption strategy, and risk assessment, validating that scaling assumptions align with identified adoption barriers and that risk mitigation strategies address actual scalability constraints
criteria.pass_condition: Must validate that scalability performance claims align with adoption timeline assumptions, ensure risk mitigation strategies address specific scalability bottlenecks identified, and verify adoption strategy accounts for performance degradation patterns under enterprise load

sub_attributes.description: Coherence between scalability assumptions and realistic adoption timelines
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if adoption timelines account for identified scalability constraints and performance impacts, 0.7 if shows some alignment between scaling and adoption, 0.4 if mentions both without coherent integration, 0.0 if treats scaling and adoption independently

sub_attributes.description: Risk mitigation strategies specifically address identified scalability bottlenecks
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if risk strategies directly target specific scalability issues with concrete mitigation approaches, 0.7 if addresses some scalability risks, 0.4 if mentions risks without scalability connection, 0.0 if ignores scalability-risk relationship

sub_attributes.description: Adoption strategy accounts for performance degradation patterns under enterprise load
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if adoption planning explicitly accounts for performance impacts and degradation patterns, 0.7 if considers some performance impacts in adoption, 0.4 if mentions performance considerations generically, 0.0 if ignores performance impact on adoption

pass_examples: Adoption strategy phases rollout to stay below 100 concurrent users until API rate limit upgrades, with risk mitigation addressing identified latency degradation through training on performance expectations
fail_examples: Proposes rapid full organization adoption while separately identifying significant performance bottlenecks under enterprise load without addressing this contradiction
criteria.research_basis: Systems thinking principles requiring coherent integration of technical constraints, organizational capabilities, and risk factors in technology adoption planning

criteria.description: Analysis of AI-generated code quality based on available research on rework rates, documented defect density studies, and identified AI-specific quality considerations like edge case handling
criteria.pass_condition: Must analyze available research showing AI code rework rates target of <5% with documented defect density comparisons to human-written code, and assess AI-specific quality considerations based on available studies and documentation

sub_attributes.description: Quantitative analysis of code churn percentage for AI vs human code
Comparative defect density analysis between AI and human code
sub_attributes.measurement: 4pts: AI equal/better, 3pts: 10% higher, 2pts: 25% higher, 1pt: >25% higher

sub_attributes.description: Assessment of AI tool's ability to identify and handle edge cases
sub_attributes.measurement: 4pts: proactive edge case detection, 3pts: adequate coverage, 2pts: basic handling, 1pt: poor edge case awareness

pass_examples: Tool shows 2.8% rework rate vs 3.3% baseline with equivalent defect density
AI code demonstrates proactive edge case handling in complex business logic
fail_examples: Tool exhibits 6.5% code churn rate with hidden edge cases requiring significant rework
AI-generated code passes tests but contains subtle bugs discovered in production
criteria.research_basis: 5.7-7.1% code churn roughly double the 3.3% baseline, with AI code hiding edge cases and subtle bugs; around 30% of developers don't trust AI-generated output

criteria.description: Evaluation against DORA AI Capabilities Model including clear AI stance, healthy data ecosystems, AI-accessible internal data, strong version control practices, working in small batches, user-centric focus, and quality internal platforms
criteria.pass_condition: Demonstrates support for at least 5 of 7 DORA AI capabilities with measurable organizational performance impact

sub_attributes.description: Tool supports clear AI stance and governance frameworks
sub_attributes.measurement: 4pts: comprehensive AI governance support, 3pts: good policy integration, 2pts: basic compliance, 1pt: minimal governance

sub_attributes.description: Enables healthy data ecosystems and AI-accessible internal data
sub_attributes.measurement: 4pts: seamless internal data integration, 3pts: good data access, 2pts: limited integration, 1pt: poor data connectivity

sub_attributes.description: Supports version control, small batches, user-centric focus, and quality platforms
sub_attributes.measurement: 4pts: excellent practice support, 3pts: good integration, 2pts: adequate support, 1pt: poor practice alignment

pass_examples: Tool integrates with internal APIs and supports incremental development with strong version control
Demonstrates measurable improvement in DORA metrics through AI capability enhancement
fail_examples: Tool operates in isolation without organizational data integration
No measurable impact on organizational performance despite individual productivity gains
criteria.research_basis: The DORA AI Capabilities Model identifies seven capabilities that determine whether AI translates to organizational performance: (1) Clear AI stance, (2) Healthy data ecosystems, (3) AI-accessible internal data, (4) Strong version control practices, (5) Working in small batches, (6) User-centric focus, and (7) Quality internal platforms

criteria.description: Evaluation of documented context window capabilities and architectural understanding features based on vendor specifications and available technical documentation
criteria.pass_condition: Must provide exact context window token limits for each tool (with target of 200,000+ tokens), specify maximum file processing capacity with documented limits (targeting 400,000-500,000 files), and identify specific cross-service dependency tracking features with technical implementation details from vendor documentation.

sub_attributes.description: Maximum token context window capacity
sub_attributes.measurement: 4pts: 200,000+ tokens, 3pts: 64,000+ tokens, 2pts: 32,000+ tokens, 1pt: <8,000 tokens

sub_attributes.description: Number of files that can be processed simultaneously
Real-time cross-service and architectural dependency understanding
sub_attributes.measurement: 4pts: real-time cross-service tracking, 3pts: good dependency mapping, 2pts: basic relationships, 1pt: single-file context only

pass_examples: Tool understands that authentication services connect to three different user management systems across 500,000 files
Provides real-time dependency tracking across microservices architecture
fail_examples: Limited to single-file context without architectural awareness
Cannot track dependencies between services or understand system relationships
criteria.research_basis: 200,000+ token context windows processing 400,000-500,000 files with real-time cross-service dependency tracking; Understanding that authentication services connect to three different user management systems is more valuable than generating comprehensive test suites without architectural awareness
